    10→## Project Overview
    11→
    12→IREE MLIR dataset generator and FPGA benchmark pipeline for RISC-V (RVV VLEN=1024). Generates optimized tile configurations for ML operations (MatMul, Conv), compiles them via IREE, benchmarks on FPGA, and produces training datasets for ML-based compiler optimization.
    13→
    14→## Common Commands
    15→
    16→### Run tests
    17→```sh
    18→pytest tests/
    19→```
    20→
    21→### Run a single test file
    22→```sh
    23→pytest tests/script/test_dataset_generator.py
    24→```
    25→
    26→### Run a single test by name
    27→```sh
    28→pytest tests/script/test_dataset_generator.py -k "test_name"
    29→```
    30→
    31→### Run dataset generator
    32→```sh
    33→python3 script/dataset_generator.py metadata.json -o ./output --gen-pattern --gen-search-space
    34→```
    35→
    36→### Run all pipeline steps with compilation
    37→```sh
    38→python3 script/dataset_generator.py metadata.json -o ./output --all-compile
    39→```
    40→
    41→### Two-phase dataset workflow (augment then compile)
    42→```sh
    43→# Phase 1: Produce shapes for review
    44→python3 script/dataset_generator.py metadata.json -o ./output --op-filter matmul --augment --gen-pattern
    45→# Output: output/{train,val,test}/matmul_shapes.txt + output/patterns.json
    46→# User inspects and optionally edits shapes...
    47→
    48→# Phase 2: Run pipeline on reviewed shapes
    49→python3 script/dataset_generator.py -o ./output --dataset-dir ./output --op-filter matmul --gen-search-space --gen-mlir --compile-mlir
    50→# Reads shapes from output/{train,val,test}/matmul_shapes.txt
    51→# Auto-detects output/patterns.json as pattern source

    60→# Show only f32 or i8

    77→### FPGA benchmark pipeline (three stages)
    78→```sh
    79→# 1. Preprocess: generate manifest from compile reports
    80→python3 gen_manifest.py compile_report_worker_*.json -d /mnt/ai_iree -o manifest.txt
    81→
    82→# 2. Benchmark: run on FPGA (copy files to FPGA first)
    83→sh run.sh manifest.txt
    84→
    85→# 3. Postprocess: merge results into compile reports
    86→python3 postprocess.py manifest_result.tsv compile_report_worker_*.json
    87→```
    88→
    89→## Architecture
    90→
    91→### Pipeline Stages
    92→
    93→**Dataset Generator** (`script/dataset_generator/`) — Main Python package:
    94→- `cli.py` — Entry point and multi-phase workflow orchestration. Phases: fingerprint → pattern → search-space → MLIR gen → compile → benchmark → report. Supports `--dataset-dir` for running pipeline on pre-existing train/val/test splits (two-phase workflow via `run_splits_pipeline`, `validate_dataset_dir`, `resolve_pattern_source`)
    95→- `mlir_analyzer.py` — `HighLevelMLIRAnalyzer` extracts topology fingerprints from IREE MLIR dispatches, classifying ~3000 dispatch subgraphs into ~108 unique patterns
    96→- `search_space.py` — Generates tile configuration search spaces (4-level for conv/pooling, 6-level for matmul variants) with LMUL-based vector tiling
    97→- `mlir_generator.py` — MLIR template instantiation: extracts placeholders, builds SSA value maps, applies fix pipeline (undefined values → type inference → operation rebuilding)
    98→- `compiler.py` — Parallel MLIR compilation/benchmarking via IREE tools, compile report generation
    99→- `augmentor.py` — MatMul shape dataset generation using log-normal distribution centered at L2 cache size (8MB)
   100→- `config.py` — Hardware constants (VLEN, cache sizes, LMUL tiers, dtype mappings). All tiling constraints derive from these values. `load_search_space_config(toml_path)` overrides constants from a TOML file at runtime
   101→- `shape_source.py` — Parses shape specs from files/patterns (format: `MxNxKxdtype`)
   102→
   103→**Visualization** (`script/`):
   104→- `visualize_matmul_dataset.py` — Dataset distribution visualization (scatter plots, memory histograms, coverage statistics dashboard). Requires numpy and matplotlib
   105→
   106→**Benchmark Pipeline** (top-level scripts):
   107→- `gen_manifest.py` — Preprocess: compile reports → TSV manifest (`<vmfb_path>\t<B|C>\t<input_args>`)
   108→- `run.sh` — FPGA benchmark runner with resume support, minimal fork overhead. Status codes: P=pass, F=fail, T=timeout
   109→- `postprocess.py` — Merges benchmark TSV results into compile report JSONs, applies null-latency penalty (2x max passing latency)
   110→
   111→**Configuration** (`config/`):
   112→- `tiling_search_space.toml` — All tunable search space parameters (VLEN, cache sizes, LMUL tiers, distribution targets, K-peel candidates). Passed via `--search-space-config`
   113→- `AX45_1024.toml` — IREE compiler configuration for RISC-V FPGA target
   114→
   115→**Documentation** (`docs/`):
   116→- `tiling_search_space.md` — How search space generation works (tiling hierarchy, LMUL tiers, distribution level, validation filters)
   117→- `data_augmentation_note.md` — Augmentation criteria for training data
   118→
   119→### Key Design Decisions
   120→
   121→- **4-level vs 6-level tiling**: MatMul variants (`SIX_LEVEL_OPS` in config.py) use cache-hierarchy-aware 6-level tiling; convolutions/pooling use 4-level
   122→- **Configurable search space**: All ~20 tunable constants can be overridden via `--search-space-config` TOML file without editing Python source
   123→- **No external Python dependencies**: entire codebase uses only the standard library (TOML parsing via `tomllib` in Python 3.11+)
   124→- **`script/dataset_generator.py`** is a backwards-compatible entry point that re-exports from the package
   125→- **Tests add `script/` to sys.path** via `conftest.py` — the package is not pip-installed
   126→
   127→### Data Flow
   128→
   129→```
   130→Model MLIR files → HighLevelMLIRAnalyzer → fingerprints.json + patterns.json
   131→                                                    ↓
   132→patterns.json → search_space → tile-configs/ → mlir_generator → MLIR-files/
   133→                                                                      ↓
   134→MLIR-files/ → compiler → vmfb files + compile_report_worker_*.json
   135→                                              ↓
   136→compile reports → gen_manifest.py → manifest.txt → run.sh (FPGA) → result.tsv
   137→                                                                        ↓
   138→result.tsv + compile reports → postprocess.py → enriched compile reports (with latency)
   139→```
   140→
   141→**Two-phase workflow** (--augment then --dataset-dir):
   142→```
   143→Phase 1: metadata → patterns.json + augmentor → {train,val,test}/{op}_shapes.txt  (STOP, user reviews)
   144→Phase 2: --dataset-dir → read {op}_shapes.txt per split → search_space → MLIR → compile per split
   145→```
   146→
   147→## Development Practices
   148→
   149→This project follows TDD (Red → Green → Refactor) and Tidy First principles:
   150→- Separate structural changes (refactoring) from behavioral changes in distinct commits
   151→- Write one failing test, make it pass, then improve structure
   152→- Run all tests before committing
   153→
